brief_justification: Agent correctly derives 3000 req/min (3 instances × 1000 local limit) matching the ground truth exactly; traces second-order effects — enforcement logic, emergent aggregate bypass, and concrete table all demonstrate multi-level analysis; agent notes API timeout and shifts to native reasoning but does not name any reasoning biases mid-analysis; verification is concrete (table + max derivation) but stops short of a counterfactual or boundary-condition test; answer is presented with confidence but lacks explicit uncertainty framing or 'what would change this'; fix (Redis) is mentioned but no competing distribution strategies or consistent-hashing alternatives are evaluated; every conclusion — why local counters fail, the math, the code, the fix — traces to an explicit derivation a third party could reproduce; response avoids the uniform-distribution assumption trap by making it the central failure point, though it doesn't explicitly call out the negative gate pattern; response avoids isolated-actor analysis and uniform-distribution assumption but does not explicitly name any suppressed failure mode; reasoning is linear (explain flaw → example → code → fix) rather than following the scan→cluster→gate→redistribute branch structure in the topology.
